# sdwan_network_hierarchy_security_logging (Resource)

This resource can manage Network Hierarchy Security Logging settings.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "sdwan_network_hierarchy_security_logging" "example" {
  high_speed_logging = [
    {
      vrf       = "1"
      server_ip = "10.1.2.1"
      port      = 2055
    }
  ]
  utd_syslog = [
    {
      vpn       = "service_lan_vpn1"
      server_ip = "10.1.1.2"
    }
  ]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `high_speed_logging` (Attributes Set) High speed logging configuration (max 4 entries) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--high_speed_logging))
- `utd_syslog` (Attributes List) UTD syslog configuration (max 1 entry) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--utd_syslog))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The id of the object
- `node_id` (String) The UUID of the Global network hierarchy node. This is automatically fetched from the SD-WAN Manager.

<a id="nestedatt--high_speed_logging"></a>
### Nested Schema for `high_speed_logging`

Required:

- `port` (Number) Server port number
  - Range: `1`-`65535`
- `server_ip` (String) Server IPv4 or IPv6 address
- `vrf` (String) VRF name or ID


<a id="nestedatt--utd_syslog"></a>
### Nested Schema for `utd_syslog`

Required:

- `server_ip` (String) Server IPv4 address
- `vpn` (String) VPN name or ID

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import sdwan_network_hierarchy_security_logging.example "f6b2c44c-693c-4763-b010-895aa3d236bd"
```
